sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Now I have a clearer picture. Let me map out the initial state:
Initial state:
- Green N container: rows 9-11, cols 28-32 (5-wide U-shape), with red dot at (10, 31)
- Yellow Y bar: rows 28-35, cols 9-11 (3-wide vertical bar), with red dot at (34, 10)
- Red diamond 1: (9,52), (10,51), (10,53), (11,52) - around center (10, 52)
- Red diamond 2: (51,10), (52,9), (52,11), (53,10) - around center (52, 10)
Gray containers:
- Top: two N-shapes (left at 20-22, 38-40; right at 20-22, 44-46)
- Bottom: two Y-shapes (top at 37-39, 23-25; bottom at 43-45, 23-25)
What I observed:
- Clicking left N-shape (20, 39): green container shrank from 5-wide to 3-wide, red dot moved from (10,31) to (10,28)
- Clicking right N-shape (21, 45): green container expanded back to 5-wide, red dot back at (10,31)
- Clicking left N-shape again: back to 3-wide
- Clicking left N-shape again: no gameplay change (just HUD)
- Clicking red dot at (10, 28): no gameplay change (just HUD)
So the two N-shapes toggle the green container between 5-wide and 3-wide. The red dot inside the container moves with it.
Now, looking at the red diamond patterns:
- Diamond 1 at (10, 52) - same row as the green container
- Diamond 2 at (52, 10) - same column as the yellow bar
Maybe the goal is to MOVE the containers to the diamond positions? The green N container needs to move to column 52, and the yellow Y bar needs to move to row 52.
But clicking the N-shapes only toggles the width, not the position. Maybe I need to click on the containers themselves to move them?
Let me try clicking on the green N container itself to see if it moves.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Maybe the goal is to move the containers to the red diamond positions
# Let me try clicking on the green N container itself to see if it moves
# The green container is at rows 9-11, cols 28-29 (currently 3-wide)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 10, 'col': 29}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
